title: Stabilize A-B end scrubbing and rebase demo3 side-control transforms
summary: 0.6.36 keeps A-B scrubbing inside the active loop window more cleanly at the boundaries and fixes the deterministic demo3 side-screen control clones so they inherit the correct relative transform data instead of carrying the middle-screen atom placement forward.
changes: Normalized scrubs now map across the active A-B range when A-B is enabled, instead of mapping across the full movie and only being clamped afterward.
Paused seeks at an active A-B boundary no longer force a StepForward refresh that can immediately bounce a very short loop endpoint.
demo3 side-screen control clones now rebase their local transform data from the middle control layout before being linked to screen_left or screen_right.
reasoning: Very short A-B loops were still behaving badly at the endpoints because the paused-frame refresh could nudge a scrubbed seek straight back into loop handling.
A-B scrubbing should use the active loop window as the meaningful seek domain for the slider, not the full media duration.
The demo3 builder was reparenting side controls correctly, but it was not rewriting the cloned atom-level transform fields to match the intended relative layout.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Scrubbing to the beginning or end of an active A-B loop should stay on that loop boundary instead of feeling unstable on a very short loop.
Left and right demo3 control copies should now sit relative to their own screens instead of carrying the middle-screen transform data.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the later incremental A-B scrub behavior yet.
This slice does not change audio, slideshow, nav polish, or broader scene layout policy.
